title: Stage names that contain variables don't work with `--target`
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

Stage names that contain a variable can't be resolved

```Dockerfile
ARG NAME="TEST"

from scratch as ${NAME}
```

```bash
$ buildah bud --target TEST
Error: The target "TEST" was not found in the provided Dockerfile
```

We encountered this issue here https://github.com/ublue-os/bluefin/pull/353

The root cause is in openshift/imagebuilder, and I already have [a fix](https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/commit/89e04944f0c8c2eb16e7a2de888ba8723080a120), but I want to clean up my code a bit before opening a PR.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.31.0-1.fc38.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.31.0
Go Version:      go1.20.5
Image Spec:      1.1.0-rc.3
Runtime Spec:    1.1.0-rc.3
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.26.1
Git Commit:      
Built:           Wed Jul  5 15:10:35 2023
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64

```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
Fedora release 38 (Thirty Eight)
NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="38.20230731.0 (Silverblue)"
ID=fedora
VERSION_ID=38
VERSION_CODENAME=""
PLATFORM_ID="platform:f38"
PRETTY_NAME="Fedora Linux 38.20230731.0 (Silverblue)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;38;2;60;110;180"
LOGO=fedora-logo-icon
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:fedoraproject:fedora:38"
DEFAULT_HOSTNAME="fedora"
HOME_URL="https://silverblue.fedoraproject.org"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://docs.fedoraproject.org/en-US/fedora-silverblue/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://ask.fedoraproject.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://github.com/fedora-silverblue/issue-tracker/issues"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_BUGZILLA_PRODUCT_VERSION=38
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="Fedora"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION=38
SUPPORT_END=2024-05-14
VARIANT="Silverblue"
VARIANT_ID=silverblue
OSTREE_VERSION='38.20230731.0'
Fedora release 38 (Thirty Eight)
Fedora release 38 (Thirty Eight)
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux fedora 6.4.6-200.fc38.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon Jul 24 20:51:12 UTC 2023 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
Hi @SoMuchForSubtlety , Thanks for creating the issue. I don't think `docker/buildkit` supports this type of instruction where `ARG` is used before creating first stage and used as an alias name. Could you confirm at your end as well if `buildkit/docker` supports this ?

While this is a nice feature request and looks useful, in past maintainers have agreed to maintain parity with `docker/buildkit` wherever possible but if other maintainers agree then following feature can be implemented and PR can be approved on imagebuilder end. @containers/buildah-maintainers PTAL

I have not tested this use-case with legacy docker but with `buildkit` I get this following error
```console
[fl@fedora docker-test]$ sudo docker buildx build --no-cache --progress=plain --target=TEST -t test .
#1 [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile
#1 transferring dockerfile:
#1 transferring dockerfile: 78B done
#1 DONE 0.3s

#2 [internal] load .dockerignore
#2 transferring context: 2B done
#2 DONE 0.3s
error: failed to solve: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = failed to solve with frontend dockerfile.v0: failed to create LLB definition: dockerfile parse error line 3: invalid name for build stage: "${NAME}", name can't start with a number or contain symbols
```

But indeed the feature requested in issue looks valuable, lets wait for maintainers to take a look.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
